name: Sendero Almoyola (PR-MU 108) — Tour from Pliego
region_intro: Hiking around Pliego offers access to a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, dry riverbeds, and areas of Mediterranean scrubland. The region features varied terrain, including paths that ascend to viewpoints and trails through agricultural areas. Limestone formations and historical elements are often encountered along the routes. Best hiking trails around Pliego The most popular hiking route is Sendero Almoyola (PR-MU 108) — Tour from Pliego, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through varied natural surroundings. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alto de la Muela – Mirador de la Cruz de Pliego loop from Pliego, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route provides panoramic views from elevated points. Local hikers also love the Castle Path – Interesting Figures loop from Mula, a 7.6 miles (12.2 km) trail leading through historical areas, often completed in about 3 hours 18 minutes. Hiking around Pliego is defined by its hills, viewpoints, and historical paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Pliego are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 570 reviews. More than 2,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Pliego's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Pliego?
A: There are over 60 hiking trails around Pliego, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. You'll find everything from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Pliego?
A: Yes, Pliego offers several easy hiking options. Approximately 23 of the trails are rated as easy, making them suitable for beginners or families looking for a more relaxed experience. One example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31400249" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mula Motorhome Area – Castillo de los Vélez loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 3.8-mile (6.2 km) route.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for hikes in Pliego?
A: Hiking trails in Pliego vary in length and elevation. Moderate routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e897698579/sendero-almoyola-pr-mu-108-tour-from-pliego" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendero Almoyola (PR-MU 108)&lt;/a&gt; can be around 8.8 miles (14.2 km) with an elevation gain of about 337 meters. Shorter, easier routes are also available, often with less significant elevation changes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pliego?
A: The best time for hiking in Pliego is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities, and the landscape is often at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler with occasional rain.

Q: Which hikes offer the best panoramic views in Pliego?
A: For panoramic views, consider routes that ascend to higher points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Muela – Mirador de la Cruz de Pliego loop from Pliego&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, specifically designed to provide extensive vistas from elevated positions.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Pliego?
A: Yes, many of the hiking routes in Pliego are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point without needing to retrace your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27630104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Muela – Mirador de la Cruz de Pliego loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10320956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle Path – Interesting Figures loop from Mula&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Pliego?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most public hiking trails in Pliego. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to follow local regulations regarding pet access.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking in Pliego?
A: While hiking in Pliego, you might encounter various Mediterranean wildlife. This can include birds of prey, small mammals like rabbits and foxes, and various reptiles. The region's diverse landscape, from scrubland to dry riverbeds, supports a range of species.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area around Pliego is rich in natural and historical attractions. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/704570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón de Alhama Summit&lt;/a&gt; for views, or historical elements such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3016870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Baños de Mula Railway Station&lt;/a&gt;. Natural formations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371620" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eroded Landscapes Trail in Barrancos de Gebas&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Pliego?
A: The hiking experience in Pliego is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 570 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, including rolling hills and historical paths, and the panoramic views from elevated points.

Q: Are permits required for hiking on trails in Pliego?
A: For the general hiking trails around Pliego, permits are typically not required. Most routes are accessible to the public. However, if you plan to visit specific protected natural areas or private lands, it's always advisable to check for any specific access rules or requirements beforehand.

Q: What kind of gear or clothing is recommended for hiking in Pliego?
A: Given the varied terrain and Mediterranean climate, it's recommended to wear sturdy hiking shoes with good grip. Layers of clothing are advisable, especially during spring and autumn, to adapt to changing temperatures. Always carry sufficient water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device.
tips:
Located in the municipality of Pliego, the Poblado de la Almoyola is an archaeological site of great importance. La Almoloya is an archaeological site belonging to the Argaric Culture. The site is known to have been occupied between 2200 and 1550 BC. and discovered in 1944.
The discoveries have allowed us to conclude that La Almoloya was a political and administrative center of the territory of El Argar.
